description: Fix scaffolded ESLint config to allow unused function parameters prefixed with underscore, matching the root monorepo convention. Without this fix, plugin developers who implement the ResourceDefinition.read(uri) interface but don't directly use the uri parameter get a lint error from their own scaffolded config.

userStories.title: Add argsIgnorePattern to scaffolded ESLint config
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the scaffolded ESLint config to allow unused function parameters prefixed with underscore so that I can implement interface-required methods like read(uri) without lint errors.
acceptanceCriteria: The scaffolded eslint.config.ts includes a rule override for @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars with argsIgnorePattern: '^_', varsIgnorePattern: '^_', and caughtErrorsIgnorePattern: '^_'
A scaffolded plugin using read(_uri: string) in a resource definition passes npm run lint
A scaffolded plugin using read(uri: string) where uri IS used still passes npm run lint
An actually-unused variable (not prefixed with _) still triggers a lint error
userStories.notes:
The scaffolded ESLint config is generated in platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts in the ESLINT_CONFIG_CONTENT constant (around line 162-179). It currently uses `...tseslint.configs.strict` which enables @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars as an error but without the underscore-prefix exception.

The root monorepo's eslint.config.ts already has this configured correctly:
```
'@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars': [
  'error',
  {
    argsIgnorePattern: '^_',
    varsIgnorePattern: '^_',
    caughtErrorsIgnorePattern: '^_',
  },
],
```

The fix is to add a rules override block to the scaffolded ESLint config that configures the same argsIgnorePattern. Add it to the existing files block that already has languageOptions/parserOptions.

The scaffolded config string is in ESLINT_CONFIG_CONTENT. Modify the `{ files: ['src/**/*.ts'], languageOptions: { ... } }` block to also include:
```
rules: {
  '@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars': [
    'error',
    {
      argsIgnorePattern: '^_',
      varsIgnorePattern: '^_',
      caughtErrorsIgnorePattern: '^_',
    },
  ],
},
```

Also update the existing test in platform/cli/src/scaffold.test.ts (if it exists) or check that the scaffold test covers this.
